note_data.title: The United States, 1919-41
keyPoints: The **Roaring Twenties** (1920s) saw unprecedented economic prosperity with mass production, consumer goods, and stock market speculation creating a boom period.
**Prohibition** (1920-33) banned alcohol production and sale, leading to organized crime, bootlegging, and widespread law-breaking that undermined government authority.
The **Stock Market Crash** of October 1929 triggered the Great Depression when share prices collapsed, wiping out millions of investors and destroying confidence.
**Mass unemployment** reached 25% by 1933 as banks failed, businesses closed, and industrial production fell by 50% from 1929 levels.
President **Hoover's policies** failed because he relied on voluntary cooperation from businesses and refused direct government intervention to help the unemployed.
**Roosevelt's New Deal** (1933-39) used alphabet agencies like the CCC, WPA, and TVA to provide jobs, relief, and economic recovery through government spending.
The **Dust Bowl** (1930s) destroyed farming in the Great Plains when drought and poor farming methods created dust storms, forcing migration to California.
**Social Security Act** (1935) established unemployment insurance and old-age pensions, creating America's first federal welfare system for workers.

definitions.definition: The constitutional ban on manufacturing, selling, and transporting alcohol in the United States from 1920 to 1933 under the 18th Amendment.
President Franklin Roosevelt's program of government intervention from 1933-39 using federal agencies and spending to combat the Great Depression.
The collapse of share prices on Wall Street in October 1929 that triggered the Great Depression and wiped out millions of investors.
The environmental disaster of the 1930s when drought and soil erosion created dust storms across the Great Plains, destroying agriculture.
New Deal programs known by their initials (CCC, WPA, TVA) that provided jobs and relief during the Great Depression.

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give 2-3 detailed points about what happened, when, and who was involved.
Give arguments for AND against, then reach a balanced conclusion with your own judgment.

modelAnswer.question: Explain why the New Deal was introduced in 1933. [6 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: The New Deal was introduced because the Great Depression had caused mass unemployment reaching 25% by 1933, leaving millions without income or hope. This was because President Hoover's policies had failed to provide adequate relief, as he refused direct government intervention and relied only on voluntary business cooperation. Additionally, the banking system had collapsed with over 5000 bank failures, destroying people's savings and confidence in the economy. Roosevelt therefore introduced the New Deal to provide immediate relief through agencies like the CCC which gave jobs to young men, recovery through programs like the TVA which stimulated economic activity, and reform through measures like the Social Security Act to prevent future depressions.
markBreakdown: Mark 5: Economic recovery through government programs
Mark 6: Long-term reform to prevent future crises

commonMistakes: Never say 'the Wall Street Crash caused the Depression' — always explain HOW it caused unemployment and business failures
Students often confuse the Stock Market Crash (October 1929) with the start of the New Deal (1933) — there was a 3-year gap
Don't just list New Deal agencies — always explain their PURPOSE (relief, recovery, or reform)
Never say Prohibition 'didn't work' — explain WHY it failed (organized crime, impossible to enforce, public opposition)
Students often ignore the social impact — always mention effects on ordinary people, families, and communities
examinerTips: This topic appears almost every year in Paper 2 — focus on causes and consequences
Always use specific statistics: 25% unemployment, 5000 bank failures, 50% drop in industrial production
Learn key dates: 1920-33 (Prohibition), October 1929 (Wall Street Crash), 1933 (New Deal begins)
For essay questions, contrast the 1920s boom with 1930s depression to show the dramatic change
Remember to evaluate — was the New Deal successful? Did it end the Depression or did World War 2?
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Timeline showing 1919-29 (Economic Boom/Roaring Twenties), October 1929 (Wall Street Crash), 1929-33 (Great Depression/Hoover's failed policies), 1933-41 (Roosevelt's New Deal), with key events and statistics marked at each stage.
